channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Bitcoin's U.S. reserve still a work-in-progress as federal agencies hash it out
item.description: The White House says it's still evaluating the "best structure" for the federal fund to hold bitcoin as a long-term reserve and a separate stockpile of other crypto assets....
item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.title: 'BusySnake' Infostealer Slithers into Critical Infrastructure Networks
item.description: A threat group researchers call "Armored Likho" has gained access to government agencies and electrical power entities in Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CitrixBleed-ing Again? NetScaler Vulnerability Under Attack
item.description: Attackers wasted little time targeting the latest memory disclosure flaw in Citrix's NetScaler products, after researchers published a proof-of-concept exploit (PoC)....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Phishing poses as big-brand job interview to steal Google accounts
item.description: A phishing campaign is impersonating more than 30 well-known brands, including Adobe, Netflix, Coca-Cola, and OpenAI, in fake job interviews to steal Google account credentials from marketing professi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake IT support calls on Microsoft Teams push EtherRAT malware
item.description: Threat actors are abusing Microsoft Teams voice calls by impersonating corporate IT support staff to trick employees into installing the EtherRAT malware, giving attackers initial access to corporate ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.description: ​Vietnamese authorities have arrested and are prosecuting seven suspects believed to have run HiAnime, the largest anime piracy streaming service before its shutdown in June. [...]...

item.title: Coinbase World Cup error shows prediction markets still have a proof problem
item.description: A disputed Coinbase sports alert shows why exchange-run prediction markets need visible provenance, verified event data, and clear accountability before automated content scales inside trading apps. T...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.title: 16-Year-Old Linux KVM Flaw Lets Guest VMs Escape to Host on Intel and AMD x86 Systems
item.description:
A use-after-free bug in Linux's KVM hypervisor can be triggered from a guest virtual machine to corrupt the shadow-page state of the host kernel that runs it.
Dubbed 'Januscape' and tracked as CVE-20...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.description: Fresh bullish signals emerged for Solana after its first SuperTrend buy trigger since October....

The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-20896 (CVSS sco...

The flaws m...

item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) said today it worked with industry partners to seize hundreds of domains associated with NetNut, a sprawling residential proxy service operated by the publicl...
item.dc:creator: Krebs On Security

item.title: FortiBleed Actors Collaborating With Inc, Lynx Ransomware Gangs
item.description: After gaining a foothold in thousands of Fortinet firewalls, the attackers are starting to monetize that access, and are also piling on a Nextcloud zero-day bug....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google Disrupts NetNut Residential Proxy Network Spanning 2 Million Home Devices
item.description:
Google has significantly degraded NetNut, one of the biggest networks that turns home devices into rented relays for other people's traffic.
Working with the FBI, Lumen, and others, Google's Threat I...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ransomware Groups Turn to Citrix Bleed 2, BYOVD, and Supply Chain Credentials
item.description:
Threat actors associated with the Anubis ransomware operation have been observed exploiting the Citrix Bleed 2 (CVE-2025-5777) vulnerability to obtain initial access.
"Although tactics differ between...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Global Schools Holdings Cites Two Injunctions in a Bid to Chill Our Reporting. It Won’t Work.
item.description: My About page is pretty clear about legal threats: If you want to send me legal threats about my reporting or comments, knock yourself out, but don’t be surprised to see me report on your threat, any ...

item.title: Ransomware Thugs Masquerade as Interpol to Entice Small Biz
item.description: The ransomware campaign relies on basic social engineering and stretches across multiple regions, including the US, Europe, Middle East, and elsewhere....

item.title: ThreatsDay: AI Compute Hijacking, Apple Email Flaw, BlueHammer Ransomware + 14 Stories
item.description:
This week’s security news is mostly about weak spots.
Browsers, bots, sandboxes, AI systems, and email flows all show the same problem in different ways. Everything looks normal until someone tests a...

item.title: Google loses final appeal to overturn €4.1 billion EU fine
item.description: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has dismissed Google's final appeal against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) antitrust fine over the company's use of Android to promote its Chrome browser a...

item.title: ConsentFix and ClickFix: How Microsoft 365 Accounts are Hijacked in 3 Seconds
item.description: ConsentFix and ClickFix attacks steal Microsoft 365 tokens in seconds using fake prompts and OAuth flows. Learn how these MFA bypass tactics work and how to defend against them. [...]...

item.title: Anthropic's AI Finds Bugs. IBM Bets $5B It Can Fix Them.

item.title: Microsoft fixes bug that removed Copilot buttons in Outlook
item.description: Microsoft has fixed a known issue causing the Copilot Chat or Copilot buttons in Classic Outlook to disappear for Windows users with the Copilot Chat (Basic) license. [...]...

item.title: Cisco finally confirms attackers exploiting Unified CM flaw
item.description: Cisco confirmed that attackers are now exploiting a Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) vulnerability patched in early June. [...]...
